headline: Two Games Fill the Last Four
deck: Spain edged Belgium to book a semi-final against France; tonight Norway meets England in Miami and Argentina meets Switzerland in Kansas City to complete the bracket. FIFA has closed the Egypt officiating row without a review, and a Portuguese referee takes the Argentina game.

art.caption: One semi-final is set; the other comes down to a single Saturday night of quarter-finals.

body: Half the semi-final is settled. Spain beat Belgium 2-1 on a late Mikel Merino goal and joins France, a 2-0 winner over Morocco, in the first semi-final [E2]. The other semi belongs to the two teams that survive tonight: Norway play England in Miami at 5pm Eastern, and Argentina play Switzerland in Kansas City at 8pm [E1].

Norway is the tournament's romance and its risk. Back at a World Cup for the first time since 1998, the Norwegians beat Brazil in the round of 16, and Erling Haaland's seven goals have made him the story and half of a Golden Boot duel with England's Harry Kane [E1]. Argentina arrive as champions and favourites, unbeaten in seven previous meetings with Switzerland, with Lionel Messi level at the top of the scoring chart on eight [E1]. Opta's model makes Argentina and England the likelier winners, which would set an Argentina-England semi; an upset in either game rewrites the board [E1].

The officiating grievance that trailed Argentina's last win has closed without a process. FIFA's refereeing chief Pierluigi Collina publicly defended the calls in the 3-2 defeat of Egypt and rejected any bias, and no formal review or disciplinary step followed; the complaint was heard and set aside [E3]. The row then took a stranger turn, with Egypt's federation confirming its email was hacked and fake messages about "corrupt" officiating sent in its name, which it is investigating [E1].

Tonight's assignment carries its own quiet history. The Argentina-Switzerland match is refereed by Portugal's João Pinheiro, consistent with a FIFA practice of keeping English officials off Argentina's games given the Malvinas past [E1]. It is the kind of detail that reads as trivia until you notice the tournament arranging itself around a forty-year-old war.

The neutral point is that a single night narrows a planet's tournament to four. What the survivors carry into next week, romance or record, grievance or ghost, is decided between kickoff in Miami and the final whistle in Kansas City [E1][E2].

evidence_box.fragment: Quarter-finals today: Norway v England (Miami) and Argentina v Switzerland (Kansas City); Haaland on seven, Messi on eight; Opta favours Argentina and England; Argentina's referee João Pinheiro (Portugal); Egypt FA confirmed an email hack
Spain 2-1 Belgium, Merino winner; Spain into a semi-final against France, who beat Morocco 2-0
FIFA's Pierluigi Collina defended the Argentina-Egypt officiating in detail and rejected bias claims; no formal review or disciplinary step opened
